title: Instantiating an interface without params fails, BLIF can't find module top
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Yosys has unit tests that instantiate SystemVerilog interfaces with parameters. When there are none the BLIF backend fails with `Can't find top module '\top'` (possibly because the hierarchy pass is removing too much, see logs at the end).

This example works `yosys -p "read_verilog -sv ./top.sv; synth_ice40 -blif out.blif"`:

```systemverilog
module top(input clk);
  MyInterface #(.PARAM(0)) MyInterfaceInstance();
endmodule

interface MyInterface #(PARAM = 0)();
  logic not_an_empty_interface;
endinterface
```

This one doesn't, there is no parameter:

```systemverilog
module top(input clk);
  MyInterface MyInterfaceInstance();
endmodule

interface MyInterface();
  logic not_an_empty_interface;
endinterface
```

## Expected behavior

Shouldn't need to add a dummy parameter and set it to a dummy value when instantiating an interface.

## Actual behavior

The BLIF backend ultimately fails with this error:

>3.49. Executing BLIF backend.
ERROR: Can't find top module `\top'!

But earlier there is a difference in the hierarchy pass.
With a param, \MyInterface gets removed but the param'd one stays:

>2.2.2. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     \MyInterface

>2.2.3. Executing AST frontend in derive mode using pre-parsed AST for module `\MyInterface'.
Parameter \PARAM = 0
Generating RTLIL representation for module `$paramod\MyInterface\PARAM=0'.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.

>2.2.4. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     \MyInterface

>2.2.5. Executing AST frontend in derive mode using pre-parsed AST for module `\MyInterface'.
Parameter \PARAM = 0
Found cached RTLIL representation for module `$paramod\MyInterface\PARAM=0'.

>2.2.6. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     $paramod\MyInterface\PARAM=0

>2.2.7. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     $paramod\MyInterface\PARAM=0
Removing unused module `\MyInterface'.
Removed 1 unused modules.

[Full log with param](https://gist.github.com/tux3/ec3dc4e668889da70bc6ef5e15ffc99a)

Without a param, \MyInterface is gone by `2.2.4`:

>2.2.2. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     \MyInterface
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\top'.

>2.2.3. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top

>2.2.4. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Removing unused module `\MyInterface'.
Removed 1 unused modules.

[Full log without param](https://gist.github.com/tux3/2acef33665b0e5a32a91cc58132d2e5a)

If you need any more information, I'll be more than happy to provide it. Thanks!
